scientificStatus: solver contract implemented; indexed experimental geometry, production impulse coupling, and measured force registration accepted; candidate A passes full-window D=8, D=12, and D=16 numerical ledgers but fails the locked 5% force-history convergence gate at 6.268%; lag, topology, accounting, and startup relaxation are rejected as dominant explanations, while a topology-free 24-bin fixed-wall canonical retains 9.961% D12/D16 impulse-history disagreement; a geometry-only D12/D16 audit clears link measure and interpolation but finds a persistent left-wing velocity-moment error, the frozen no-fluid velocity A/B rejects solid-node sampling, and a source-exact localization finds only 8/7 sparse outliers; the exact ray-root A/B proves all 15 links interpolate endpoint distances from different components, and the preregistered coefficient bound finds 10 q=0.5 branch changes with a 3.189 maximum normalized operator L1 change; only a frozen captured-population replay is authorized, D=20 and production changes remain blocked, and source-viscosity agreement plus same-specimen biological free-flight input remain open

missing: complete body surface or registered body radii
whole-bird mass and principal inertia for this specimen
bilateral wing mass, center of mass, and inertia

rejectedHybridCandidates.source: Read et al. hummingbird turning dataset
rejectedHybridCandidates.reason: marker kinematics belong to different birds and cannot complete the Maeda specimen

rejectedHybridCandidates.source: Macaulay et al. hummingbird body mass-distribution model
rejectedHybridCandidates.reason: a different specimen/model cannot supply same-specimen body inertia for Maeda kinematics
verified archive contains reference-curve MATLAB inputs, not raw reconstructed complete-bird geometry

rejectedHybridCandidates.source: Bruce Museum American-crow entire-individual CT volumes on MorphoSource
rejectedHybridCandidates.reason: the preserved specimens are tightly folded and are not the Jackson and Dial flight subjects; they can support anatomy or explicitly hybrid sensitivity geometry but cannot close same-specimen flight surface, kinematics, mass, or inertia

alternativeExperimentalValidation.source: Deetjen et al. Ringneck-dove synchronized surface, kinematics, and aerodynamic-force dataset
alternativeExperimentalValidation.reasonFreeFlightRemainsBlocked: the source assumes bilateral surface symmetry and models the distributed wing mass; same-specimen bilateral wing and registered whole-bird inertia are not measured
alternativeExperimentalValidation.antiFabricationRule: derived lateral force, per-wing loads, or modeled inertia must not be relabeled as experimental measurements
alternativeExperimentalValidation.nextAction: capture production reflected, auxiliary, previous-incoming, wall-projection, and density primitives for the 15 links at the frozen D12 phase, then replay production-q versus exact-global-q populations and momentum exchange offline; do not implement a boundary correction, run D20, or relax the raw spatial gate

releaseDecision.blockingCondition: provide one schema-2 dataset whose complete geometry, bilateral kinematics, whole-bird inertia, and bilateral wing mass properties all identify the same specimen
releaseDecision.nextAction: continue same-specimen data requests independently while running one frozen D12 captured-primitive population and momentum replay on the 15 coefficient-sensitive links; D20 and production promotion remain blocked because the 24-bin fixed-wall history remains 9.961% and realized force sensitivity is not yet established
releaseDecision.antiFabricationRule: do not substitute another specimen's body, mass, inertia, tail, or contralateral wing and call the result measured
